subject: defconfig: update.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: exFAT filesystem volume dirty flag synchronization is lazy instead of eager; mitigates potential data-loss scenarios in power-fail during file operations on SD card

notes:
CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC is a Samsung exfat driver feature that does not exist in vanilla 6.18 Kconfig. This commit (on MiSTer-v5.14 branch only) toggles it from disabled to enabled in the fork's v5.15 defconfig. Vanilla 6.18's exfat driver lacks this option entirely. Our 6.18 linux.config does not include CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC even though stock-linux.config has it enabled=y; this is expected since our mainline exfat lacks the feature. The Samsung exfat symlinks are carried via patch 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, but that patch does not include the DELAYED_SYNC codepaths (which were part of the fork's Samsung driver implementation). No userspace code references this option. Disposition: dropped-deliberate — the fork feature was not forward-ported to 6.18's mainline exfat.

sonnet-verified: PASS, no amendment. `grep -rn EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC /mnt/source/linux` returns zero hits anywhere in the vanilla 6.18.38 tree (fs/exfat/Kconfig only defines EXFAT_FS and EXFAT_DEFAULT_IOCHARSET) -- confirmed absent as claimed. Re-derived the commit's own diff via git show: it flips `# CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC is not set` to `CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC=y` in the fork's MiSTer_defconfig, and `git branch -a --contains` confirms this commit exists only on origin/MiSTer-v5.14, matching the record's source_branch field. Also confirmed stock-linux.config:3511 has CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC=y (stock did carry it) while both board/mister/de10nano/linux.config and the resolved output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config lack the symbol entirely (grep returns nothing in either) -- consistent with 'not forward-ported' rather than 'forward-ported but disabled'. 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch confirmed to contain no reference to delayed sync.
